title: aion-proto crate scaffold and tonic-build wiring
purpose: Create the aion-proto leaf crate — the shared wire contract every client SDK, worker SDK, and the server depend on. Wire up the proto-compilation toolchain so later briefs add .proto files and get generated Rust types. This is the foundation of the entire wire layer.
task: Create the aion-proto crate as a workspace member depending only on aion-core plus external crates (prost, tonic, serde, and tonic-build as a build dependency). Add a build.rs that compiles the crate's proto/ directory via tonic-build, and a thin lib.rs that includes the generated code and re-exports it alongside the hand-written serde/convert/error modules added in later briefs. Empty placeholder proto compilation is acceptable here; the message families land in AW-002..AW-005.

requirements.title: Crate scaffold and workspace wiring
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL create the aion-proto crate as a workspace member with a lib.rs that contains only module declarations and re-exports. THE SYSTEM SHALL declare dependencies on aion-core and external crates only, with no dependency on aion, aion-server, or any store backend.
acceptance: crates/aion-proto exists and is registered in the workspace Cargo.toml members list.
aion-proto/Cargo.toml declares aion-core and external crates (prost, tonic, serde) plus tonic-build under [build-dependencies] — and no aion-* dependency other than aion-core.
src/lib.rs contains only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports, no logic.
The crate sets unsafe_code = "deny" and inherits the workspace clippy lints.

requirements.title: tonic-build proto compilation
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL add a build.rs that compiles every .proto under the crate's proto/ directory via tonic-build, emitting generated Rust into OUT_DIR. THE SYSTEM SHALL include the generated code through lib.rs.
acceptance: build.rs invokes tonic-build (or prost-build via tonic-build) over the proto/ directory and reruns when a .proto changes (cargo:rerun-if-changed).
lib.rs includes the generated module(s) via include!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), ...)) behind a generated module, kept to declarations and re-exports.
A minimal committed .proto (or the AW-002 common.proto if landed first) compiles cleanly so the build succeeds.
cargo build -p aion-proto succeeds and the generated types are reachable from the crate root.

requirements.title: Lint and format conformance
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL ensure the crate passes the workspace lint and format gates, including the generated-code inclusion, without any lint-bypass directives.
acceptance: cargo clippy -p aion-proto --all-targets -- -D warnings passes (generated code isolated so it does not require crate-wide #[allow]).
cargo fmt --check passes for hand-written code.
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives appear in hand-written code.
The generated module is confined to a single included file so lint scope is contained.

requirements.title: Contract documentation header
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL document, at the crate root, that aion-proto is the single shared wire contract consumed by aion-server, all client SDKs (AL), and all worker SDKs (AR), and that it depends only on aion-core to keep the wire Event identical to the domain Event.
acceptance: lib.rs has a crate-level doc comment stating the contract role and the aion-core-only Aion dependency (per design D1, D2).
The doc names the four message families to be added (workflow management, event streaming, worker protocol, wire errors) so the crate's intended shape is discoverable.
missing_docs produces no warnings for the public surface introduced here.

boundaries: SHALL NOT define the workflow, events, or worker message families — those land in AW-003, AW-004, AW-005.
SHALL NOT define the WireError taxonomy or proto<->core conversions — that is AW-002.
SHALL NOT add any dependency on aion, aion-server, or a store backend (leaf-crate constraint CO5).
SHALL NOT create the aion-server crate — that is AW-006.
verification: aion-proto/Cargo.toml lists no Aion dependency other than aion-core.
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in hand-written code.
